title: Irritable infant with bulging fontanelle
presentation: A 7-month-old male is brought in by his mother for 24 hours of fever, irritability, and poor feeding. He cried inconsolably when picked up. No sick contacts. Immunizations are up to date. Vitals: Temp 39.4C, HR 168, RR 44, BP 88/54, SpO2 97%. Exam: bulging anterior fontanelle, paradoxical irritability, mottled skin, no rash.

urgent: CSF cell count glucose protein culture
gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Bulging fontanelle + paradoxical irritability + fever in infant = bacterial meningitis until proven otherwise. Antibiotics should not be delayed for LP in unstable infant.
